title: May 2026 in review
summary: No May result changed the LEV outlook. The useful trial read was mostly absence of results: several watched studies were complete or near expected completion with no posted outcomes in available public records, while the field still lacked repeated human evidence that aging-relevant health or function improves for longer.
lede: May did not produce a result that changed the LEV picture. The trial picture was mostly open questions rather than new evidence: watched studies in sirolimus, urolithin A, wound repair, metformin, emtricitabine, and elamipretide still needed posted outcomes or linked publications.
bottom_line: LEV did not look closer after May. The field still needed repeated human studies showing long-lasting improvements in health or function.
field_change_note: No May result moved the overall stage, firmness of the read, or LEV timing outlook. The evidence still depended on early human signals, biomarkers, trial listings, and narrow functional or disease contexts.
bullets: No May result changed the overall LEV conclusion.
Several watched trials had no posted outcomes in available records, including completed or near-completion studies in wound repair, urolithin A, metformin, and emtricitabine.
Immune rejuvenation remained one of the more clinically legible areas, but not proof of broad aging reversal.
Senescence and mitophagy did not produce May results that changed the overall conclusion.

current_context.label: Rapalogs
current_context.summary: The sirolimus older-adults trial listing was last updated February 3, 2026 and was recruiting with no posted results. That left rapalogs visible but not yet stronger.

current_context.label: Immune rejuvenation
current_context.summary: The track remained easier to connect to human endpoints than many hallmark areas, but May did not add evidence that immune benefits generalize into broad aging reversal.

current_context.label: Cellular senescence
current_context.summary: Senolytics remained a clear intervention thesis, but May did not resolve the central gap: human functional outcomes were still sparse and uneven.

current_context.label: Mitophagy
current_context.summary: Mitophagy enhancers had human trial narratives and biomarker signals, but the clinical meaning of mitochondrial biomarker gains remained unsettled.

current_context.label: NAD and redox context
current_context.summary: A June 9 review added MIB-626 target-engagement and physiology records, no-results NR/NMN/MIB-626/MitoQ registries, and recent review context. That made the mitochondrial evidence picture easier to judge, but it did not change the May conclusion.

what_did_not_change: No hallmark crossed into long-lasting disease or mortality relevance.
The overall field still lacked repeated human studies showing long-lasting improvements in health or function.
Biomarkers, trial activity, and narrow functional or disease signals still carried more of the evidence than broad aging outcomes.
No credible LEV-relevant window could be assigned from the May evidence.
why_it_matters: May mainly reinforced the prior conclusion: several tracks were worth monitoring, but none reported results strong enough to make LEV look closer.

trial_horizon.label: EPA+DHA wound-healing trial
trial_horizon.summary: This older-adult chronic venous leg ulcer trial completed Dec. 18, 2024, and the Jun. 2, 2026 ClinicalTrials.gov API view still had no posted results. A result could matter for repair and inflammation tracks, but the absence of posted outcomes was not a May field advance.

trial_horizon.label: Sirolimus older-adults trial
trial_horizon.summary: The trial was recruiting as of the Feb. 3, 2026 registry update, with no posted results and no completion date in the public record. That means May had no clear sirolimus result expectation; it remained a watch item.

trial_horizon.label: Urolithin A in frail older adults
trial_horizon.summary: This registry-linked study was complete by Apr. 25, 2025, but the Jun. 2, 2026 ClinicalTrials.gov API view had no posted results. It was a concrete no-result watch item, not a May field advance.

trial_horizon.label: ANTHEM metformin aging study
trial_horizon.summary: The ANTHEM registry listed completion on Mar. 12, 2025 and no posted results as of its Apr. 23, 2026 update. That made it a metformin aging-biomarker result to watch, not evidence that metformin looked stronger in May.

trial_horizon.label: LINE-AD emtricitabine
trial_horizon.summary: The registry record listed Mar. 31, 2026 as the study end date and had no posted results in the Jun. 6, 2026 API view. A near-term registry or publication update would be useful, but it would still be Alzheimer-specific rather than broad aging proof.

trial_horizon.label: SHAPE elamipretide pilot
trial_horizon.summary: The registry listed Apr. 15, 2026 as the estimated study end date, and the Jun. 2, 2026 record version had no posted results. This was near-term mitochondrial trial context, but the small open-label design means even a result would need cautious interpretation.

signals_to_watch.label: Function from older-adult studies
signals_to_watch.summary: The next useful result would pair a pathway change with something clinically legible in older adults: mobility, frailty, wound closure, cognition, immune function, or safety.

signals_to_watch.label: Biomarkers that line up with function
signals_to_watch.summary: Metformin, urolithin A, and elamipretide would matter more if biomarker changes lined up with strength, endurance, wound repair, cognition, or another functional readout.

signals_to_watch.label: Senolytics with human outcomes
signals_to_watch.summary: The key senescence question is whether clearing senescent cells leads to better physical function, slower disease progression, or acceptable safety over months.

evidence_gaps.label: Function, not just markers
evidence_gaps.summary: The strongest missing evidence was still repeated human improvement in health or function.

evidence_gaps.label: Duration
evidence_gaps.summary: Short-term signals still needed follow-up long enough to show whether benefits last.

evidence_gaps.label: General aging relevance
evidence_gaps.summary: Disease-specific or pathway-specific results still needed clearer links to aging itself.

track_examples.label: Visible human testing
track_examples.title: Rapalogs
track_examples.summary: The key concrete item was a recruiting older-adult sirolimus trial listing with no posted results.

track_examples.label: Clinically legible
track_examples.title: Immune rejuvenation
track_examples.summary: Human endpoints are more legible here than in many areas, but broad aging reversal remains unproven.

track_examples.label: Promising but uneven
track_examples.title: Cellular senescence
track_examples.summary: A strong intervention thesis still limited by sparse and uneven human functional outcomes.

track_examples.label: Biomarker boundary
track_examples.title: Mitophagy enhancers
track_examples.summary: Human trial narratives exist, but mitochondrial biomarker gains still need clearer clinical meaning.

reader_takeaways: May did not materially change the LEV picture.
The strongest areas to watch were still specific and evidence-limited.
A recruiting trial listing is not proof.
The next meaningful update would need actual human results that last.
review_reason: Post-publication editorial rollup after the June 9 NAD/redox missing-context update confirmed that the May 2026 retrospective conclusion remains unchanged; no result or outlook change was added to the covered period.

revision_history.summary: Converted the edition into the canonical retrospective May 2026 review, removed tracker-coverage framing, and later expanded the trial horizon with concrete watched no-result and not-yet-expected trial records without changing the May conclusion.

Reviewed the June 7 exPDite-2 activity-only registry refresh against this May 2026 edition. It happened after the covered period and did not change the May conclusion, so the edition received no new field-change item.

Reviewed the June 9 NAD/redox missing-context update against this May 2026 edition. It added MIB-626 target-engagement and physiology records, no-result registries, and review context, but no May result changed the period conclusion.
